title: Podman Windows 11 mounting secret fails because of mismatched path separators
body:
### Issue Description

When building a container on Windows 11, mismatched path separators in a `podman-secret-XXXXXXXXXX` file path cause the build command to fail. I believe that it is caused by `os.CreateTemp` in [this function](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/blob/1f94d50d46e0aed93539cfd28f606d1298f1f1b5/internal/remote_build_helpers/utils.go#L49) but I cannot find where it's being called.

It seems like `os.CreateTemp` is using the path separator for the host system (Windows 11) rather than the machine (WSL 2).

Here is the `Containerfile` I'm using:
```Dockerfile
ARG NODE_VERSION=24
ARG PORT=8080

FROM node:${NODE_VERSION}-alpine AS base
WORKDIR /app

FROM base AS deps
COPY package.json package-lock.json .npmrc ./
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mysecret \
    MY_SECRET="$(cat /run/secrets/mysecret)" npm ci

FROM deps AS dev
ARG PORT
ENV NITRO_PORT=${PORT}
ENV NITRO_HOST=0.0.0.0
EXPOSE ${PORT}
CMD ["npm", "run", "dev"]

FROM deps AS build
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM base AS runtime
ARG PORT
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NITRO_PORT=${PORT}
ENV NITRO_HOST=0.0.0.0
COPY --from=build /app/.output ./.output
USER node
EXPOSE ${PORT}
CMD ["node", ".output/server/index.mjs"]
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. On Windows 11 Powershell, run:
```sh
podman machine init
podman machine start
```
2. Then, build the container with:
```sh
podman build -t myproject:dev -f Containerfile --target dev --secret id=mysecret,env=MY_SECRET . && podman run --rm -it -p ${PORT:-8080}:8080 -v "$PWD":/app -v /app/node_modules myproject:dev
```


### Describe the results you received

It fails with:
```
Error: creating temp file: open /mnt/c/Users/MyUsername/Projects/myproject\podman-build-secret-XXXXXXXXXX: The system cannot find the path specified.
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the command to succeed.
